content.full_text:
May 29, 1990

California Fair Political Practice Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear FPPC:

I have read Section 85800-85807 of the Political Reform Act of 1974 as amended to January 1, 1990 and I am confused as to what I am to do with funds left over from the April 10, 1990 Campaign for City Clerk of the City of Isleton.

I was informed in March 1990 that I would have to open a checking account if I intended to spend any money on the campaign.

The minimum amount for a checking account at the local bank is $100.00. I wrote a personal check for $100.00 and deposited it to my campaign.

I spent $3.75 having 25 Xeroxed Posters made to put at various locations around town. I was re-elected.

Now, what I want to know is what I am legally required to do with the $96.25 that is still in the account.

I was informed by your office that I could keep it for the next election campaign in 1994, or I could donate it the charity.

None of these seems very practical to me as the position only pays $25.00 per month or $300.00 per year.

It seems to me that your are trying to penalize the small city candidate for public office.

The $96.25 is not the point of the letter, it is a matter of principal to me that you can interpret the INTENT of the law to penalize a person who is only trying to do their city responsibilities. I have been re-elected seven times since I was appointed City Clerk in 1965, so some people must have confidence in me.

Your legal opinion on this matter will also affect some of the local Councilmembers campaign funds.

Thank you for taking the time to research this matter.

Yours for Honest Government,

John E. Golden
City Clerk
